chunks.page_summary: This document details the integration of Rheonics Smart Module Electronics (SME) 4-20mA analog output channels with an Allen Bradley 1794-IE8 module, including wiring guidelines and default measurement parameters for SRV and SRD sensors.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Analog Signal Integration with Allen Bradley 1794-IE8 Module

Modified on: Tue, 22 Oct, 2024 at 3:53 PM

Which Rheonics products are related to this Article?
This Article is based on the use of the SME (Smart Module Electronics) from SRV (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/), SRD (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/), DVP (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-gas-lng-density-meter-dvp/) and DVM (https://rheonics.com/products/hpht-pvt-density-meter-viscometer-dvm/). Hence it can be used for any of these Rheonics sensors.

What is this Article helpful for?
This article helps with the correct wiring to use SME's 4-20mA Channels and read them correctly with the Allen Bradley's 1798-IE8 Analog Input Modul and similars.

1. SME 4-20mA Channels Overview

Rheonics SME (Smart Module Electronics) has 3 in-built Output Channels for 4-20mA measurements. These are labeled in the SME housing as CH1+, CH2+ and CH3+.

Figure 1: SME Analog Output Channels.

By default, these 3 Channels send the following Parameter for the SRV and SRD sensors.

Table 1: Default Measurements for SRV.
| Channel | Default Measurement SRV
|---|---
| 1 | Viscosity Median and Last Good
| 2 | Density Median and Last Good
| 3 | Temperature Median

Table 2: Default Measurements for SRD.
| Channel | Default Measurement SRD
|---|---
| 1 | Density Median and Last Good
| 2 | Viscosity Median and Last Good
| 3 | Temperature Median

Follow the Analog signals setup with the RCP (https://support.rheonics.com/support/solutions/articles/81000403437-analog-signals-setup-with-the-rcp) article to change the Channel-Parameter relations and parameterize the 4-20mA Outputs.
The SME should be considered as a 4-wire transmitter. By definition, a 4-wire transmitter needs 2 wires connected to a power supply and 2 signal wires are used by a reading Instrument (e.g. a PLC, Multimeter, Ammeter). From these two last wires, one is the Analog Signal and the other is the reference wire to close the Circuit, as shown in Figure 2 below.

Figure 2: 4-wire Transmitter Example Diagram.

The SME has 3 non-isolated Current Outputs, which means they share the same reference. To operate, the SME requires an external Voltage Supply (24V DC) through 2 wires. To read through the 4-20mA Channels, each of the three Analog Signals has a unique port (CH1, CH2, CH3) and two other ports are used as references (gnd-). The Figure below shows a possible wiring connection to read the SME's Analog Channels.
key_facts: This article is relevant for Rheonics SRV, SRD, DVP, and DVM sensors when used with the SME.
The article helps with the correct wiring to use SME's 4-20mA Channels and read them correctly with the Allen Bradley's 1798-IE8 Analog Input Modul and similar devices.
Rheonics SME (Smart Module Electronics) has 3 in-built output channels for 4-20mA measurements, labeled CH1+, CH2+, and CH3+ on the housing.
By default, for SRV sensors, Channel 1 outputs Viscosity Median and Last Good, Channel 2 outputs Density Median and Last Good, and Channel 3 outputs Temperature Median.
By default, for SRD sensors, Channel 1 outputs Density Median and Last Good, Channel 2 outputs Viscosity Median and Last Good, and Channel 3 outputs Temperature Median.
The SME should be considered a 4-wire transmitter.
A 4-wire transmitter requires 2 wires for power supply and 2 signal wires for reading.
The SME has 3 non-isolated Current Outputs, meaning they share the same reference.
The SME requires an external 24V DC Voltage Supply.
Each of the three Analog Signals (CH1, CH2, CH3) has a unique port, with two other ports used as references (gnd-).

Notice how two Measuring Devices are connected externally, but internally all three shared the same “gnd-”. It is always important to understand the wiring diagram of the Measuring Device (especially of PLCs reading Modules) to assure a good reading.

2. Integration with Allen Bradley 1794-IE8 AI Module

This Input Module is part of a FLEX I/O System from Allen Bradley. The system, shown in the Figure below, is completed with a Terminal Base, where physical wiring with a sensor is made, and an Adapter, that enables communication with any other Allen Bradley controller and also supplies Voltage for the I/O Module.

Adapter
Terminal base
I/O module

Figure 4: Allen Bradley's FLEX I/O System [1].

Follow Allen Bradley Documents for the correct hardware connection between the AI Module, the Terminal Base, and the Adapter.
A Terminal Base used for 1794-IE8 Module is the 1794-TB3. It has 51 terminals, divided in three rows: A, B and C. Figure below shows this distribution and examples for 4, 3,
and 2-Wire Transmitters wiring.

key_facts: Rheonics SME analog channels are connected to measuring devices, with all three internally sharing the same "gnd-".
The Allen Bradley 1794-IE8 Input Module is part of a FLEX I/O System.
The FLEX I/O System comprises a Terminal Base for physical wiring, an I/O Module (sensor), and an Adapter for communication and voltage supply.
The specific Terminal Base for the 1794-IE8 Module is the 1794-TB3.
The 1794-TB3 terminal base has 51 terminals, organized into three rows labeled A, B, and C.
diagram_labels: Figure 3: SME Analog Channels Example Diagram.
Figure 4: Allen Bradley's FLEX I/O System [1].
Figure 5: Connection Diagram for 4-, 3- and 2-Wire Transmitters [2].
warnings_notes: It is always important to understand the wiring diagram of the Measuring Device (especially of PLCs reading Modules) to assure a good reading.
Follow Allen Bradley Documents for the correct hardware connection between the AI Module, the Terminal Base, and the Adapter.

chunks.page_summary: This page details the wire connections for the Allen-Bradley 1794-IE8 analog input module, specifically for use with Rheonics SME devices, and lists terminal assignments for current and voltage signals.
chunks.extracted_text:
Figure 6: 1894-IE8 mounted on a Terminal Base [3].

It can be seen that each one of the 8 Analog Input Channels has a Terminal for Current (I) and Voltage (V). For Rheonics SME only Terminals for Current (I) should be used. The complete list of Channel's Terminals is presented below:

Wire Connections for 1794-TB2, and 1794-TB3 using the 1794-IE8 Module
Channel | Signal Type | Label Markings | 1794-TB2 and 1794-TB3 Input | Common Terminal
Input 0 | Current | I | A-0 | B-17
Input 0 | Voltage | V | A-1 | B-18
Input 1 | Current | I | A-2 | B-19
Input 1 | Voltage | V | A-3 | B-20
Input 2 | Current | I | A-4 | B-21
Input 2 | Voltage | V | A-5 | B-22
Input 3 | Current | I | A-6 | B-23
Input 3 | Voltage | V | A-7 | B-24
Input 4 | Current | I | A-8 | B-25
Input 4 | Voltage | V | A-9 | B-26
Input 5 | Current | I | A-10 | B-27
Input 5 | Voltage | V | A-11 | B-28
Input 6 | Current | I | A-12 | B-29
Input 6 | Voltage | V | A-13 | B-30
Input 7 | Current | I | A-14 | B-31
Input 7 | Voltage | V | A-15 | B-32
24V DC common | | | B-16...33(1) |
+24V DC power | | | 1794-TB2 – C-34...51 |
+24V DC power | | | 1794-TB3 – C-34...51 |

(1) Terminals 16...33 are internally connected in the terminal base unit.

Figure 7: Channels Ports for 1794-TB3 on 1794-IE8 [1].

3. SME Wiring Connection to Allen Bradley's 1794-IE8

A connection example between the 1794-IE8 Analog Module and SME is represented in the wiring diagram below.
key_facts: The 1794-IE8 module has 8 Analog Input Channels.
Each analog input channel on the 1794-IE8 module has terminals for both Current (I) and Voltage (V) signals.
For Rheonics SME applications, only the Current (I) terminals of the 1794-IE8 module should be used.
Terminals 16 through 33 on the terminal base unit are internally connected.
A wiring diagram illustrating the connection between the 1794-IE8 Analog Module and SME will be provided.
warnings_notes: For Rheonics SME only Terminals for Current (I) should be used.
Terminals 16...33 are internally connected in the terminal base unit.

chunks.page_summary: This page illustrates the connection diagram between an SME device and an Allen-Bradley Flex I/O 1794-IE8 Analog Input module, providing detailed wiring instructions and a note on considerations for 4-20mA channel usage.

Figure 8: SME and 1794-IE8 Connection Diagram.

In this example, CH1+ from SME is connected to Current Input 0 (Pin 0) from the AI Module, CH2+ is connected to Input 1 (Pin 2) and CH3+ to Input 2 (Pin 4). Notice how the 2 reference pins (gnd-) from the SME are connected to pin 17 and 19, in accordance with Figure 7, but this could be a redundancy. Terminal Base 1794-TB3 has all pins from 16 to 33 (row B) internally connected, therefore, wiring only one "gnd-" port from the SME to a pin from row B would be enough.

Note: When using 4-20mA Channels take in consideration factors that can affect the readings: What can go wrong with 4-20mA Loops? https://support.rheonics.com/support/so

References:
[1] https://literature.rockwellautomation.com/idc/groups/literature/documents/um/1794-um062_-en-p.pdf
[2] https://literature.rockwellautomation.com/idc/groups/literature/documents/in/1794-in100_-en-p.pdf
[3] https://www.amazon.com/-/es/Bradley-1794-ie8-entrada-anal%C3%B3gica-1794-Tb3/dp/B01M0VTGBN https://www.rockwellautomation.com/en-us/products/hardware/allen-bradley/i-o/in-cabinet-modular-i-o/1794-flex-i-o-i-o-modules.html
key_facts: CH1+ from SME is connected to Current Input 0 (Pin 0) from the AI Module.
CH2+ from SME is connected to Input 1 (Pin 2) from the AI Module.
CH3+ from SME is connected to Input 2 (Pin 4) from the AI Module.
Two gnd- reference pins from the SME are connected to pins 17 and 19 of the AI Module, although this might be redundant.
Terminal Base 1794-TB3 has all pins from 16 to 33 (row B) internally connected.
Wiring only one "gnd-" port from the SME to any pin in row B of the 1794-TB3 is sufficient due to internal connections.
A connection diagram for SME and 1794-IE8 is provided as Figure 8.
warnings_notes: Note: When using 4-20mA Channels take in consideration factors that can affect the readings: What can go wrong with 4-20mA Loops? (https://support.rheonics.com/support/so)
